# BetterPortal v10 - LLM Onboarding

A multi-tenant portal platform composed of independent services unified by a theme. API-first, HTMX-first, strict schema validation, plugin-based.

> **Protocol-first.** BetterPortal is a **wire protocol** (`bp-protocol/1`). The normative spec lives in [`spec/`](spec/README.md). `framework/nodejs` is the **Node SDK** - one reference implementation. A PHP/Python/Go/Rust service that implements the spec is a first-class BetterPortal service.
>
> This file is the working guide for the Node SDK and the current repo. When this file disagrees with the spec on protocol behavior, **the spec wins** - file a bug.
>
> | Source of truth | What |
> |---|---|
> | [`spec/README.md`](spec/README.md) | Index of all protocol docs. |
> | [`spec/protocol.md`](spec/protocol.md) | HTTP surface, CORS, content negotiation. |
> | [`spec/manifest.md`](spec/manifest.md) | `/.well-known/bp/manifest`. |
> | [`spec/schema-json.md`](spec/schema-json.md) | `/.well-known/bp/schema.json`. |
> | [`spec/fragment-html.md`](spec/fragment-html.md) | Fragment HTML rules + `HX-Trigger` events. |
> | [`spec/config.md`](spec/config.md) | `bp-config.yaml` + per-service config endpoints. |
> | [`spec/auth.md`](spec/auth.md) | OIDC + JWT + JWKS + config tickets. |
> | [`spec/sse.md`](spec/sse.md) | `/__sse` convention + control-plane sync. |
> | [`spec/streaming.md`](spec/streaming.md) | Partial responses: frame envelope, NDJSON, per-frame validation, streamed HTML. Node SDK: `createStreamHandler` + `index.stream.tsx` (example: hello-view `bp-routes/delayed/`). |
> | [`spec/search.md`](spec/search.md) | Federated search: `search.v1` provider endpoint, pinned result schema, aggregator rules. |
> | [`spec/conformance.md`](spec/conformance.md) | Test matrix. |
>
> This document covers everything the Node SDK adds on top: file-based routing, codegen, BPService base class, theme runtime, admin tooling. Most of it is implementation guidance, not protocol.

---

## 1. Architecture (top-down)

```
                       +-------------+
                       |   Browser   |
                       +--+--+---+---+
            shell + nav   |  |   |  HTMX fragments / SSE
                +---------+  |   |  (CORS, direct)
                v            |   v
        +--------------+     |   +-------------+
        |   Theme(s)   |     |   |  Service A  |
        |  port 3100   |     |   |  port 3200  |
        |  bootstrap1  |     |   +-------------+
        +--------------+     |   +-------------+
                             +-->|  Service B  |
                                 |  port 3300  |
                                 +-------------+

  Theme serves only the HTML shell + nav/style/brand/fragment-list
  endpoints. Each service is its own origin; the browser calls them
  directly via HTMX. The theme NEVER proxies content from a service.

                          +-----------------+
                          | bp-config.yaml  | -- source of truth
                          | (file-backed    |     for tenants / apps /
                          |  platform store)|     routes / services /
                          +-----------------+     menus / fragments
```

### Roles

- **Theme** (`themes/nodejs/bootstrap1/`): renders the host page (HTML shell) + acts as the front-end origin. Resolves tenant/app from hostname. Provides nav/brand/style/fragments endpoints. Owns the visible URL bar. Does **not** call backend services itself - the browser does.
- **Service** (`services/nodejs/.../`): self-contained BSB plugin. Exposes typed routes via file-based convention. Negotiates JSON or HTML representation. Owns its own data and config.
- **Config Manager** (`services/nodejs/admin/config-manager`): special service. Mutates `bp-config.yaml` via REST. Hosts admin views (services registry, tenants, routes, menu editor, fragments editor, theme designer dispatch).
- **Node SDK** (`framework/nodejs/`, npm: `@betterportal/framework`): TypeScript implementation of `bp-protocol/1`. Contracts, runtime helpers, codegen (`bp-codegen`), h3 adapter, schema validation. Imported by all Node services and themes. **Other-language SDKs would live as sibling packages** (`@betterportal/sdk-php`, etc.) - none exist yet.
- **BSB integration plugin** (`plugins/nodejs/betterportal-bsb`, npm: `@betterportal/plugin-bsb`): wires h3 + CORS + observability into BSB. Provides `BPService` base class for service plugins. Node-only by definition.
- **Auth services** (`services/nodejs/auth-default/`, `services/nodejs/auth-authress-io/`): optional shared auth provider services. Apps choose a provider through `app.auth.serviceId`, which points at a tenant service id or shared-service activation id, never directly at a plugin id.

### Communication rules

- **Browser -> Theme**: full-page HTML and assets.
- **Browser -> Service**: HTMX requests for content swaps and fragments. CORS allows methods `GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS` and exposes HX-* headers.
- **Theme -> Service**: only during server-side resolution (manifest lookups, schema discovery) - never to fetch page content. Theme renders the shell only.
- **Service -> Service**: not standard. Use platform services or events.
- **Config Manager -> Service**: not allowed as an architectural assumption. Config-manager cannot assume network reachability to services. Service metadata arrives via service -> config-manager sync; config read/write/schema UI uses browser -> service requests with CP-signed config tickets.

---

## 1b. Design constraints (read this before adding anything)

These are non-negotiable. The whole architecture is built on them.

- **No iframes.** Ever. Content composition is HTMX fragment swaps, not nested browsing contexts. Iframes break theming, navigation, accessibility, cookies, CSS variables, and the URL bar.
- **Service route tokens, not hardcoded service paths.** Service-rendered HTML may use `{view.id}` in `href`, `action`, `hx-get`, `hx-post`, `hx-put`, `hx-patch`, `hx-delete`, and `hx-download`. The framework rewrites those tokens server-side to service route paths. Do not use this for browser-visible UI/app routes; the theme/shell owns those.
- **No SPA framework.** No React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, etc. JSX-htmx is server-side template syntax that emits strings - it is not a client runtime.
- **No client-side router.** URL state is server-driven via `hx-push-url`. Browser back/forward "just works" because htmx pushes real history entries.
- **No client-side state library.** No Redux, Zustand, etc. State lives in: (a) the server, (b) URL/query, (c) HTML attributes on DOM elements. That is enough.
- **No client-side data-fetching library.** No fetch wrappers, no SWR, no React Query. HTMX is the network layer.
- **HTML-as-API.** Services emit HTML fragments. The browser inserts them. No client code re-parses or transforms response bodies.
- **Service-declared route chrome.** Routes may export flat primitive `chrome`, e.g. `export const chrome = { fullScreen: true }`. Values must be `string | number | boolean`. Codegen emits it into the view manifest; service sync copies it onto app routes unless the app route explicitly overrides it. The framework also serializes chrome onto HTML response content types as `bp-chrome-*` params (`bp-chrome-full-screen=true`), so themes can react after HTMX swaps. Themes should read route/response chrome, not infer full-screen state from auth service ids.
- **Route dependencies and API-only views.** Keep one route model: use `apps[].routes[]` for renderable views and API/dependency views. `index.ts` may export `dependencies = ["other.viewId"]`; codegen also detects literal `{view.id}` route tokens in renderer files and merges them into dependencies. Config-manager auto-adds dependency routes when the parent is mounted. Non-renderable/API routes are managed in Route Designer with UI-only fields disabled.
- **Service-to-service contracts.** Services should not hardcode target service ids for module/provider cases. Providers declare `apiContracts`; callers declare `m2mRequests` by contract id/version/capabilities. Config-manager stores explicit `m2m.bindings` and `m2m.grants`; provisioning identity alone grants no M2M API access.
- **Stable view ids.** Routes may export `viewId = "stable.id"`. Use explicit view ids for anything referenced by app config, permissions, dependencies, or code. File-derived ids are defaults only and change when route files move. Codegen/build must reject duplicate view ids.
- **Manifest-owned route metadata.** Route `methods`, service path (`targetPath`/resolved path), renderability, dependencies, and default chrome come from the service manifest. Config-manager sync reconciles those fields and disables routes whose viewId disappeared. Do not make HTTP methods editable in the UI; users control mount path/title/query only for renderable routes.
- **Route URL helpers.** In handlers, use `ctx.routeUrl(viewId, { params, query, absolute })` for service-facing URLs; absolute URLs use the service hostname/base URL. Use `ctx.uiRouteUrl(viewId, { params, query, absolute })` for browser-visible app routes; absolute URLs use the app hostname/base URL. Do not manually scan `ctx.app.routes` against `ctx.serviceId`; app routes store tenant-service/shared-activation UUIDs while handlers usually know the plugin id.
- **Authenticated downloads.** Use `hx-download="/service/path"` for BP-authenticated file/binary downloads. The active theme rewrites the URL to the owning service, attaches fresh BP headers at request time, fetches the blob, applies BP header directives, and saves using `Content-Disposition` or the `download` attribute. Do not put static `hx-headers` in service HTML for downloads.
- **Raw/file routes.** Use `createRawHandler()` for API-only endpoints that return `Response`/files/streams. Raw routes must not export `ResponseSchema` or theme renderers; codegen forces them non-renderable. Use `ctx.response(body, init)` for custom responses and `ctx.file(body, { filename, contentType, size, disposition })` for downloads. Raw routes still run auth, app allowlist, input validation, tracing, `routeUrl`, and BP header directives.
- **Multipart uploads.** Multipart is opt-in via exported `MultipartSchema` and `createRawHandler({ multipart: MultipartSchema }, ...)`. `ctx.multipart.fields` contains string fields, `ctx.multipart.files` contains buffered `{ fieldName, filename, contentType, size, data }` files; repeated keys become arrays. Buffered multipart is capped at 25 MiB total file bytes and returns `413` when exceeded; add streaming/resumable protocols only for proven large upload needs.
- **Automation discovery.** Themes expose `GET /.well-known/bp/public` with `protocol`, `tenantId`, `appId`, `tenantUrl`, `configManagerUrl`, and `catalogUrl`. Config-manager exposes `GET /.well-known/bp/automation/catalog?tenantUrl=...` returning services, cached config schemas, and cached service actions from manifest sync. Config-manager must not fetch services server-side for automation discovery. Node-RED, n8n, and Zapier scaffolds live under `automations/` and share `automations/shared/bp-client.js`.
- **AI discovery.** Themes expose `GET /.well-known/bp/ai.json`, `GET /llms.txt`, and HTML discovery tags (`rel="llms"`, `betterportal:ai-manifest`). Start from the tenant app URL, read the AI manifest, then use the automation catalog for business/service actions. For app management tasks, use the discovered management app URL and config-manager `/.well-known/bp/manage/*` endpoints. Platform admin is operator-only and AI agents must not use it for user tasks.
- **Management API.** Config-manager exposes `GET /.well-known/bp/management` plus `/.well-known/bp/manage/current`, `services`, `services/activate`, `routes`, `fragments`, and `theme`. These endpoints resolve the current app from explicit `appId`/`tenantUrl` first, then `Referer`/`Origin` context. Raw UUIDv7 ids are intentionally visible for API, automation, and AI use.
- **Webhooks.** Services declare webhook events in their manifest; users never edit payload schemas. Handlers emit with `await ctx.webhook?.("event.id", payload)`. Config-manager stores delivery targets, receives service events at `POST /.well-known/bp/webhooks/events`, signs JSON POST deliveries, skips disabled tenants, and retries up to target `maxAttempts`. User/app management uses `/.well-known/bp/manage/webhooks/events` and `/.well-known/bp/manage/webhooks/targets`; platform admin uses `/.well-known/bp/admin/webhooks/*`. File delivery is single-process; add Postgres claiming only for real multi-container delivery.
- **BP header directives.** Clients, automation tools, and AI agents must process `BP-SetHeader` and `BP-RemoveHeader`. Store BP headers until expiry, remove them when directed, and send fresh live headers on later BP API calls. `Referer` and `Origin` help context resolution but are not auth; prefer discovered URLs, `tenantUrl`, `appId`, and BP headers.
- **Shared service sync metadata.** Scoped sync exposes shared activations in `tenant.services` with public data only: activation id, plugin/catalog service id, hostname/baseUrl, title/description, capabilities/tags, logo/category, deployment mode, and `source: "shared"`. Never put shared service secrets in scoped sync.
- **Each service is its own origin.** Browser hits services directly (`http://localhost:3200`, etc.). Theme does not proxy traffic. This forces CORS-correctness and enforces service isolation.
- **All cross-service request validation is schema-first.** Inputs validated on entry; outputs validated on exit. `anyvali` everywhere.
- **Cookies are theme-origin only.** See section 15. Cross-origin cookies do not work for service requests. Browser-managed BP headers are set with `BP-SetHeader` / `BP-RemoveHeader` and owned by the shell.
- **Live-refresh via `HX-Trigger` events.** Mutations emit an event header; passive regions subscribe via `hx-trigger="event from:body"`. No websockets needed for typical UI.
- **SSE for server-push.** SSE handler convention is `GET.sse.ts` + `_<loc>.<frag>.GET.sse.tsx`. WebSockets need framework-level connection lifecycle support and are not route files.
- **Codegen is mandatory.** Route registration is file-system driven through `bp-codegen`. Hand-written registries are not supported.
- **BSB package shape.** Runnable services/themes are BSB plugins: `@bsb/base`, `@betterportal/framework`, and `@betterportal/plugin-bsb` are runtime dependencies. `@bsb/tests` is dev-only and used through `"test": "bsb-plugin-cli test"` for packages that want shared BSB plugin checks. Keep `tests/`, `*.test.ts`, and `bsb-tests.json` out of published package `files`.
- **Strict separation of admin and tenant data.** Tenant-scoped operations only see their tenant's services + apps. Platform admin operations are gated behind config-manager.

If a tempting feature requires breaking any of these (e.g., "let's just iframe an external site"), the answer is: no, build a service binding or a fragment.

---

## 2. Repository layout

```
BetterPortal/
+-- bp-config.yaml                    # platform config (tenants/apps/routes/services/menu/fragments)
+-- framework/nodejs/                 # core contracts, runtime, codegen, h3 adapter
+-- plugins/nodejs/betterportal-bsb/  # BSB<->framework integration (BPService base class)
+-- themes/nodejs/
|   +-- bootstrap1/                   # full Bootstrap 5 + HTMX theme
|   +-- embedded/                     # lightweight embedded theme
+-- services/nodejs/
|   +-- auth-default/                 # default JWT auth provider service
|   +-- auth-authress-io/             # Authress provider service
|   +-- examples/hello-view/          # example business service
|   +-- admin/config-manager/         # admin service (mutates bp-config.yaml)
+-- docs/                             # architecture + decision records
```

Each service / theme is a BSB plugin published as an npm package and run independently. Multiple services run concurrently on different ports.

---

## 3. Core concepts

| Concept             | Description                                                                                                                                                  |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Tenant**          | Top-level isolation boundary. Owns its own services + branding.                                                                                              |
| **App**             | A site/product under a tenant. Has theme, routes, menu, fragments. Tied to one or more hostnames.                                                            |
| **Route**           | Maps a public URL path to a service+view. Has `path`, `serviceId`, `viewId`, `targetPath` (service-side URL).                                                |
| **Service**         | BSB plugin exposing views + manifest. Registered as a direct tenant service or activated from the shared service catalog.                                     |
| **Shared service**  | Platform-managed provider registered in `sharedServiceCatalog` and bound through `sharedServiceActivations`. Apps reference the activation id.                |
| **View**            | A route directory under `bp-routes/<dir>/`. `index.ts` is metadata; `GET.ts`, `POST.ts`, etc. are method handlers.                                            |
| **Theme renderer**  | Method/status-specific HTML file under `_theme.<themeId>/`, e.g. `GET.tsx`, `POST.422.tsx`, `422.tsx`.                                                       |
| **Fragment**        | HTML island rendered inline at a named location. File: `_<location>.<fragmentId>.METHOD.tsx`. Pulled via `?_f=location.fragmentId`.                           |
| **Component**       | Composable named region inside a view. File: `<componentId>.METHOD.tsx`. Pulled via `?_c=componentId`.                                                       |
| **Menu**            | Tree of `{type, title, routeId|href, children}` per app. Drives sidebar nav. Items: `link`, `group`, `external`, `section`, `divider`.                       |
| **Slot**            | Legacy fragment binding (deprecated - use `app.fragments` instead).                                                                                          |
| **Config schema**   | Per-service descriptor declaring tenant- or app-scoped config fields. Surfaced via `/.well-known/bp/config/schema`.                                          |

Bootstrap registers config-manager as the direct control-plane tenant service. Default auth and Bootstrap1 are shared catalog entries with activations for the admin tenant/app. App references (`shell.serviceId`, `auth.serviceId`, routes, fragments, slots, role grants) use tenant service ids or shared activation ids only. When migrating a tenant service to shared, rewrite references from the old tenant service id to the activation id; never point app references at a plugin id or shared catalog id.

Service configuration is independent from service usage. A tenant service or shared activation can be configured for the tenant before routes, shell, fragments, or auth bindings use it. The config editor defaults to tenant values; app scope is an override layer where unchecked fields fall back to tenant defaults. Runtime scoped apps may stay usage-filtered, but scoped config must include `configApps` for every app that may be configured.

Shared service creation is URL-first like normal service registration. The browser loads `/.well-known/bp/manifest` from the service and posts the derived plugin id/title/category/capabilities/deployment modes to config-manager. Config-manager must not fetch service manifests server-side.

---

## 4. Well-known endpoints (per service)

Every BPService exposes these automatically:

| Path                                | Purpose                                                                |
| ----------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `GET /.well-known/bp/manifest`      | Plugin id, title, version, views (with schemas).                       |
| `GET /.well-known/bp/health`        | `{ ok: true, plugin, port }`.                                          |
| `GET /.well-known/bp/schema.json`   | Manifest + flattened route list (paths, themes, fragments, components).|
| `GET /.well-known/bp/config/schema` | Config schema descriptor for service + whether custom UI exists.       |
| `GET /.well-known/bp/config`        | Read tenant/app config values (config API token via `Authorization: Bearer ...`). |
| `POST /.well-known/bp/config`       | Write tenant/app config values (config API token via `Authorization: Bearer ...`). |

Themes additionally expose:

| Path                                    | Purpose                                                  |
| --------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| `GET /.well-known/bp/theme/style`       | Reloadable CSS fragment (listens for `bp:theme-changed`).|
| `GET /.well-known/bp/theme/brand`       | Brand text fragment.                                     |
| `GET /.well-known/bp/theme/nav`         | Sidebar nav HTML for current app.                        |
| `GET /.well-known/bp/theme/fragments?location=X` | Fragment list HTML for a shell location.        |
| `GET /.well-known/bp/config/ui`         | Theme designer page (custom UI).                         |

---

## 5. File-based routing convention

A service's routes are scanned from the directory declared in `package.json`:

```json
"betterportal": {
  "routes": ["src/plugins/<plugin-name>/bp-routes/"]
}
```

For a route `/hello`:

```
bp-routes/
  hello/
    index.ts                         # route metadata only
    GET.ts                           # GET schemas + default handler
    POST.ts                          # POST schemas + default handler
    GET.sse.ts                       # optional SSE handler
    _theme.bootstrap1/
      GET.tsx                        # GET page HTML for bootstrap1
      POST.422.tsx                   # POST 422 HTML
      _nav.profile.GET.tsx           # fragment at location "nav", id "profile"
      _nav.profile.GET.sse.tsx       # optional SSE tick renderer
      showcase-cards.GET.tsx         # named component (queryable via ?_c=showcase-cards)
    _theme.embedded/
      GET.tsx                        # embedded GET page
```

Rules:
- Directory name -> URL path (use `[param]` for required params, `[[param]]` for optional params, `_theme.X` is special).
- `index.ts` is required at the leaf and is metadata-only.
- Method handlers are `GET.ts`, `POST.ts`, `PUT.ts`, `PATCH.ts`, `DELETE.ts`, `OPTIONS.ts`; each default-exports a handler and exports its schemas.
- Renderers are method/status-specific. No generic `index.tsx` fallback exists.
- Fragments: `_<location>.<fragmentId>.METHOD.tsx`.
- Components: `<componentId>.METHOD.tsx`.

Path params have two layers. Service route files use bracketed folders, e.g. `bp-routes/tenants/[tenantId]/services/index.ts`, which codegen registers as `/tenants/:tenantId/services` and exposes as `ctx.params.tenantId`. Optional folders use double brackets, e.g. `bp-routes/tenants/[[tenantId]]/services/index.ts`, which registers both `/tenants/services` and `/tenants/:tenantId/services`; `ctx.params.tenantId` is absent on the list route. App route config may use `{tenantId}` in `path` and `targetPath` (legacy `:tenantId` also works); the theme/app route layer matches the visible UI path and interpolates those params into the service target path. Do not re-derive these values from `event.req.url` inside view files.

HTMX lane rule: service-rendered content must not expand its swap target outside its lane. Main content may target `#bp-main` or content-owned overlays; fragment content may target only itself or descendants inside its own `data-bp-fragment` container. Never let service HTML target `body`, theme nav/menu, or unrelated fragments. The bootstrap shell sanitizes incoming `hx-target` values, blocks request-time escapes, and normalizes service `HX-Location` targets to `#bp-main`.

After **adding/removing** files, run codegen to regenerate the registry:

```
npx bp-codegen
# or
node <repo>/framework/nodejs/lib/codegen/cli.js
```

Codegen writes `.bp-generated/registry.ts`. The service plugin imports `registry` from there.

`bsb-plugin-cli build` does NOT auto-run codegen. Adding `bp-codegen` to a `prebuild` script is recommended.

---

## 6. Anatomy of a service plugin

```
services/nodejs/examples/hello-view/
+-- package.json                                        # bsb + betterportal config
+-- sec-config.yaml                                     # BSB runtime config
+-- tsconfig.json
+-- src/plugins/service-betterportal-hello-view/
    +-- index.ts                                        # Plugin class (extends BPService)
    +-- bp-routes/                                      # routes scanned by codegen
    |   +-- hello/...
    +-- .bp-generated/
        +-- registry.ts                                 # GENERATED - never edit
```

### Plugin class skeleton

```ts
import { type BSBServiceConstructor, createConfigSchema, createEventSchemas } from "@bsb/base";
import * as av from "anyvali";
import { BetterPortalConfigSchema, BPService, type BPServiceDefinition } from "@betterportal/plugin-bsb";
import { registry } from "./.bp-generated/registry.js";

const PluginConfigSchema = av.object({
  host: av.string().minLength(1).default("0.0.0.0"),
  port: av.int().min(1).default(3200),
  // Optional: omit for setup-mode/control-plane bootstrap.
  betterportal: BetterPortalConfigSchema
}, { unknownKeys: "strip" });

const Config = createConfigSchema(
  { name: "service-betterportal-myservice", description: "...", tags: [...] },
  PluginConfigSchema
);

const EventSchemas = createEventSchemas({
  emitEvents: {}, onEvents: {},
  emitReturnableEvents: {}, onReturnableEvents: {},
  emitBroadcast: {}, onBroadcast: {}
});

export class Plugin extends BPService<InstanceType<typeof Config>, typeof EventSchemas> {
  static Config = Config;
  static EventSchemas = EventSchemas;

  constructor(cfg: BSBServiceConstructor<InstanceType<typeof Config>, typeof EventSchemas>) {
    super({ ...cfg, eventSchemas: EventSchemas });
  }

  protected definition(): BPServiceDefinition {
    return {
      manifest: {
        pluginId: "service.betterportal.myservice",
        title: "My Service",
        description: "...",
        configSchemas: [/* per-tenant/per-app config field descriptors */]
      },
      registry
    };
  }
}

export { Config, EventSchemas };
```

`BPService` from `@betterportal/plugin-bsb` handles: HTTP server, h3 routing, CORS, observability wiring, registering all routes from `registry`, and the well-known endpoints.

`this.config` is fully typed from `PluginConfigSchema` (inferred through `BPService<InstanceType<typeof Config>, ...>`) - access fields directly, never cast it. Use `av.x().default(v)` for fields with defaults (type stays required); reserve `av.optional(...)` for fields that may genuinely be absent (type becomes `T | undefined`).

For services that need custom endpoints beyond routes, extend `BSBService` directly or call `this.app.get/post/...` inside `onRegistered`.

---

## 7. Anatomy of a route (`bp-routes/<dir>/`)

```ts
// index.ts - metadata only
import type { ApiAuthRequirement, CacheHints, DemoScenario } from "@betterportal/framework";

export const viewId = "hello.index";
export const title = "Hello View";
export const description = "...";
export const auth: ApiAuthRequirement = { required: false, permissions: [] };
export const cacheHints: CacheHints = { ttlSeconds: 60, varyBy: ["accept", "origin"] };
export const dependencies = ["hello.detail.index"];
```

```ts
// GET.ts - method schemas + default handler
import * as av from "anyvali";
import type { Infer } from "anyvali";
import { createHandler } from "../.bp-generated/route-runtime.js";

export const QuerySchema = av.object({ name: av.string().default("World") }, { unknownKeys: "strip" });
export const HeadersSchema = av.object({}, { unknownKeys: "strip" });
export const RequestSchema = av.object({}, { unknownKeys: "strip" });
export const ResponseSchema = av.object({
  greeting: av.string().minLength(1)
}, { unknownKeys: "strip" });
export type ResponseData = Infer<typeof ResponseSchema>;

export default createHandler(
  { response: ResponseSchema, query: QuerySchema },
  (ctx) => ({
    greeting: `Hello, ${ctx.query.name}`
  })
);
```

`createHandler` enforces:
- `ctx.query` is `Infer<QuerySchema>` (already parsed)
- Return type matches `Infer<ResponseSchema>` (TS-checked)
- Runtime: re-parses output through `ResponseSchema` (belt + braces)

Typed handler context:
- Route files that only need validated input/output can import `createHandler`, `createRawHandler`, or `createStreamHandler` from `@betterportal/framework`.
- Route files that need `ctx.plugin` or BP service config should import factories from the generated local runtime, e.g. `../.bp-generated/route-runtime.js` with the correct relative path.
- Codegen types that runtime from the plugin `index.ts`: `export class Plugin` becomes the `ctx.plugin` type, and `export type ServiceConfig = ...` becomes the `ctx.config` type.
- `ctx.config` is BetterPortal-managed service config resolved for the current tenant/app scope. It is not the BSB plugin startup config (`this.config`).
- Do not cast `ctx.plugin` or `ctx.config` in route files when the generated runtime can provide the type.

Method files are service API boundaries. They validate inputs, build typed response models, and stay independent of browser-facing theme state. Do not infer UI paths, selected tabs/tenants, or public hostnames inside method files from `event.req.url` or `Host`; UI path/query state belongs in method/status-specific renderers or must be passed as explicit schema data. UI paths are not service paths, and the UI hostname is not the service hostname.

Content negotiation is automatic:
- `Accept: application/json` -> returns JSON body
- `Accept: text/html` -> invokes the theme renderer for the negotiated theme
- HTMX requests get fragment-mode HTML (no surrounding shell)

For mutations, add `POST.ts`, `PUT.ts`, `DELETE.ts`, or `PATCH.ts`.

For population from h3 middleware (e.g., injecting platform state):

```ts
this.app.use("/services", (event) => this.populateContext(event));
```

Inside the populate, set `(event as any).__bpResponseModel = data`. The handler reads it back via `ctx.rawEvent.__bpResponseModel`.

---

## 8. Anatomy of a theme view (`_theme.bootstrap1/GET.tsx`)

```tsx
/** @jsxImportSource jsx-htmx */
import type { HtmlRenderable } from "@betterportal/framework";
import type { ResponseData } from "../GET.js";

export function render(data: ResponseData): HtmlRenderable {
  return (
    <section class="container-fluid">
      <h1>{data.greeting}</h1>
      <button hx-get="{hello.detail.index}" hx-target="#out" hx-swap="innerHTML">Load</button>
      <div id="out"></div>
    </section>
  );
}
```

Rules:
- `/** @jsxImportSource jsx-htmx */` is required.
- Import `ResponseData` from the matching method file or a shared helper.
- Return JSX (string-based, server-rendered).
- Bootstrap 5 + HTMX attributes only - no client framework.
- Use `{view.id}` route tokens for service routes. The framework rewrites supported attributes server-side. UI/app routes remain the theme/shell's responsibility.
- To opt **out** of URL rewriting (rare - theme-shell elements only), add `data-bp-no-route=""`.
- For SPA-style links, use anchor tags with relative `href`. The shell upgrades them to HTMX boosted nav automatically when in-tenant.

---

## 9. Anatomy of a fragment (`_<location>.<fragmentId>.GET.tsx`)

```tsx
/** @jsxImportSource jsx-htmx */
import type { HtmlRenderable } from "@betterportal/framework";
import type { ResponseData } from "../GET.js";

export function render(data: ResponseData): HtmlRenderable {
  return <div class="...">...</div>;
}
```

Accessed via `GET <serviceUrl>/<routePath>?_f=<location>.<fragmentId>`. The theme inserts a placeholder `<div hx-get=".../?_f=nav.profile" hx-trigger="load">` for each binding declared in `app.fragments.<location>`.

To trigger live re-render of a fragment region, respond from another endpoint with `HX-Trigger: bp:fragments-changed` (or whatever event the theme region listens for) and ensure CORS exposes `HX-Trigger`. The theme's shell region wraps fragments with `hx-trigger="bp:fragments-changed from:body"` and re-fetches `/.well-known/bp/theme/fragments?location=<loc>`.

---

## 10. `bp-config.yaml` - platform source of truth

```yaml
themes:
  - id: bootstrap1
    hostname: http://localhost:3100
    title: Bootstrap 1
    enabled: true

platformServices:
  - id: bp-auth
    hostname: http://localhost:3400
    apiKeyHash: <sha256>
    serviceId: service.betterportal.auth-default
    category: auth
    enabled: true

tenants:
  - id: betterportal
    slug: betterportal
    title: BetterPortal
    active: true
    branding: { brandName: BetterPortal }
    services:
      - id: hello-view
        hostname: http://localhost:3200
        apiKeyHash: <sha256>             # "" allowed in dev
        serviceId: service.betterportal.hello-view
        deploymentMode: self-hosted
        enabled: true
    activatedPlatformServices: []            # legacy platformServices[] only

sharedServiceCatalog:
  - id: service.betterportal.auth.default
    serviceId: service.betterportal.auth.default
    title: BetterPortal Default Auth
    baseUrl: http://localhost:3210
    apiKeyHash: <sha256>
    category: auth
    tags: [auth]
    enabled: true
sharedServiceActivations:
  - id: auth-default-admin
    tenantId: betterportal
    sharedServiceId: service.betterportal.auth.default
    enabled: true

apps:
  - id: betterportal-web
    tenantId: betterportal
    slug: web
    hostnames: [localhost:3100]          # request host -> resolves to this app
    themeId: bootstrap1
    themeConfig: { mode: system, bootstrap: {...}, light: {...}, dark: {...} }
    defaultRoute: /
    routes:
      - id: hello
        path: /                          # public URL
        serviceId: hello-view            # references tenant.services[].id
        viewId: hello.index              # <directory>.<file> in bp-routes
        targetPath: /hello               # actual service URL
        title: Hello
        enabled: true
        methods: [GET]
    menu:                                # tree drives sidebar nav
      - id: m-hello
        type: link
        title: Hello
        routeId: hello
        enabled: true
      - id: m-settings
        type: group
        title: Settings
        enabled: true
        children:
          - { id: m-services, type: link, title: Services, routeId: settings-services, enabled: true }
    fragments:
      nav:                               # location -> list of fragments
        - serviceId: hello-view
          fragmentId: profile
          targetPath: /hello
          enabled: true
      footer: []
    slots: []                            # legacy, prefer fragments
```

Rules:
- `apps[].hostnames` determines which app a request resolves to (matched against `Host` header).
- `routes[].targetPath` can include query string (`/hello?_c=showcase-cards`) to render a specific component.
- Menu `type: "group"` only nests one level deep (children must be `link` / `external` / `section` / `divider`). Groups cannot nest groups.
- `fragments` only renders shell regions the theme declares (bootstrap1 supports `nav` topbar + `footer`).

Read/written by `FileBackedBetterPortalConfigProvider` (themes) and `FileBackedPlatformConfigStore` (config-manager). Schema validated on every read via Zod-like `av.object()` from `anyvali`.

---

## 11. `sec-config.yaml` - BSB runtime config (per service)

```yaml
default:
  observable:
    observable-default: { plugin: observable-default, enabled: true, config: {} }
  events:
    events-default: { plugin: events-default, enabled: true }
  services:
    service-betterportal-hello-view:
      plugin: service-betterportal-hello-view
      enabled: true
      config:
        host: 0.0.0.0
        port: 3200
        betterportal:
          bpConfigPath: ../../../../bp-config.yaml
          # Dev only: static config-API fallback secret. Inert unless the env var
          # BP_ALLOW_DEV_CONFIG_TOKEN=true is also set. In normal (CP-synced)
          # operation, services verify CP-signed config tickets via the CP JWKS
          # and need no token. Never set this (or the env var) in production.
          configApiToken: bp-dev-config-token
          configEncryptionKey: bp-dev-encryption-key-min16chars
```

`config` fields must match the `PluginConfigSchema` declared in the service's `index.ts`. BSB validates on startup.
Use `betterportal.bpConfigPath` for local development only. In production, services should normally start without `betterportal.controlPlaneUrl` or `betterportal.serviceApiKey`; they enter setup mode and learn/persist control-plane bootstrap state during browser-driven install. Do not put service API keys in compose by default.

Coolify deploys use `docker-compose.coolify.yaml`: config-manager needs `BP_CP_ISSUER` set to its public URL, while non-CM services persist `.bp-bootstrap` and `.bp-sync-cache` in volumes after install.

---

## 12. Build & run

```bash
# install (workspace-aware)
npm install

# build framework + plugins (TypeScript)
cd framework/nodejs && npm run build
cd plugins/nodejs/betterportal-bsb && npm run build

# build a service
cd services/nodejs/examples/hello-view
npx bp-codegen                       # regen .bp-generated/registry.ts if routes changed
npm run build                        # bsb-plugin-cli build (compiles to lib/)

# run a service
npm run start                        # bsb-plugin-cli start - reads sec-config.yaml

# dev mode with reload
npm run dev
```

Standard ports (dev):
- `3100` - bootstrap1 theme (front-end origin)
- `3200` - hello-view example service
- `3300` - config-manager admin service
- `3400` - auth-default (optional)

To bring everything up:
```bash
cd themes/nodejs/bootstrap1 && npm run start &
cd services/nodejs/examples/hello-view && npm run start &
cd services/nodejs/admin/config-manager && npm run start &
```

Wait for each `/.well-known/bp/manifest` to return 200.

---

## 13. HTMX patterns used throughout

### 13a. Cross-origin requests + CORS

All services use `BPService` which configures CORS via `handleCorsRequest`:
- `methods: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS`
- `allowHeaders: Accept, Authorization, Content-Type, HX-* family, X-BP-Tenant-Id, X-BP-App-Id`
- `exposeHeaders: HX-Trigger, HX-Trigger-After-Swap, HX-Location, HX-Push-Url, HX-Redirect, HX-Refresh, HX-Replace-Url, HX-Reswap, HX-Retarget`

If you add a custom HX-* header to a response, ensure it's in `exposeHeaders` (in `plugins/nodejs/betterportal-bsb/src/service.ts`) or the browser can't read it cross-origin.

### 13b. URL rewriting (theme runtime)

`themes/nodejs/bootstrap1/src/plugins/.../assets.ts -> resolveServiceLinks` runs after every HTMX swap:
- Walks all newly inserted elements with `a[href], [hx-get|post|put|patch|delete], script[src], img[src], link[rel=stylesheet]`.
- Looks up the closest `[data-bp-service]` ancestor -> maps to that service's origin from `data-bp-services="..."` on the shell root.
- Rewrites root-relative paths (`/foo`) to absolute (`http://localhost:3200/foo`).
- Skips elements with `data-bp-no-route=""` - use this on theme-shell elements that always target the theme itself (style, brand, nav, fragments wrappers).

Always write hx-* paths as relative. Never hardcode `http://localhost:3300/...`.

### 13c. Live refresh via HX-Trigger

Pattern for "save in component X -> refresh component Y":

```ts
// Service-side: save endpoint returns HX-Trigger header
return htmlResponse(refreshedFormHtml, 200, "text/html; mode=fragment", {
  "HX-Trigger": "bp:menu-changed"
});
```

```tsx
// Component Y in a method renderer (rendered once on initial page load):
<nav id="bp-nav-desktop"
  hx-get="/.well-known/bp/theme/nav"
  hx-trigger="bp:menu-changed from:body"
  hx-swap="innerHTML"
  data-bp-no-route="">
  {renderNavItems(navItems)}
</nav>
```

Events used in this codebase: `bp:theme-changed`, `bp:menu-changed`, `bp:fragments-changed`, `bp:config-saved`.

### 13d. Hidden-form pattern (for JS-triggered HTMX)

When JS computes a payload (drag-drop, custom event) and needs to submit via HTMX:

```html
<form id="bp-action" style="display:none"
  hx-post="/some/endpoint" hx-target="#region" hx-swap="outerHTML">
  <input type="hidden" name="x" />
  <input type="hidden" name="y" />
</form>
```

```js
form.querySelector("[name=x]").value = ...;
form.requestSubmit();  // htmx intercepts the submit event
```

Form's relative hx-post gets rewritten by `resolveServiceLinks` to the correct origin. Avoids hardcoding URLs in JS.

### 13e. Idempotent body-delegated handlers

Long-lived UI bindings (drag, keyboard) go on `document.body` once, gated by a dataset flag:

```js
if (document.body.dataset.bpFooBound !== "1") {
  document.body.dataset.bpFooBound = "1";
  document.body.addEventListener("...", handler);
}
```

Survives HTMX swaps; never double-binds.

---

## 14. Per-service config (tenant/app scoped)

Each service can declare `configSchemas: ConfigSchemaDescriptor[]` in its manifest:

```ts
{
  id: "myservice.tenant",
  title: "Tenant Config",
  scope: "tenant",                 // or "app"
  jsonSchema: { ... },
  groups: [
    { id: "connection", title: "Connection", order: 10, optional: false }
  ],
  fields: [
    { key: "apiKey", title: "API Key",
      scope: "tenant",
      visibility: "secret",        // "secret" | "protected" | "public"
      ownership: "plugin",         // "plugin" | "bp" | "mixed"
      sourceOfTruth: "plugin",     // "plugin" | "bp"
      groupId: "connection",
      order: 10,
      defaultValue: "",
      required: false }
  ]
}
```

Surfaced by each service at `GET /.well-known/bp/config/schema`. Editable from `/settings/services` through a browser-mediated flow: config-manager mints a short-lived CP-signed config ticket, then the browser calls the target service directly for schema/read/write. Do **not** implement config-manager server-side fetch/proxy calls to service config/schema endpoints; remote/private services may be reachable from the browser but not from config-manager.

Config field metadata is service-declared and generic. Use `groups[]` + `field.groupId` for grouped fields, `field.order` for deterministic display, `field.defaultValue` for schema defaults, and `group.optional` when app-scope UI should allow clearing/enabling a related set of overrides together. Use optional `field.ui` hints for generated controls: `control` can be `text`, `textarea`, `password`, `number`, `checkbox`, `select`, `multiselect`, `color`, `date`, `time`, `datetime-local`, `url`, or `email`; `options` supplies select choices; `placeholder`, `min`, `max`, `step`, and `rows` are native input hints. UI hints are not validation. Do not hardcode provider-specific config forms in config-manager when manifest metadata can express the layout.

For custom UI (full-page editor instead of generated form):
```ts
registerServiceConfigRoutes({
  app: this.app,
  serviceId: "...",
  configSchemas: [...],
  mode: "hybrid",
  customUiPath: "/.well-known/bp/config/ui",   // service handles GET on this path
  ...
});
```

Storage options provided by framework:
- `InMemoryServiceConfigStore` - dev only.
- `FileBackedServiceConfigStore` - encrypts `visibility: "secret"` fields with AES-256-GCM using `configEncryptionKey`.

---

## 15. Auth

Auth in BetterPortal is **opt-in** per tenant and built on **bearer tokens, not cookies**. Themes and services compose the auth flow themselves using framework primitives.

### Why not cookies?

Each service runs on its own origin (`localhost:3200`, `localhost:3300`, etc.). The theme runs on yet another (`localhost:3100`). Browser cookies do not cross origins - a cookie set by the theme is not sent to a service. SameSite=None+Secure only widens 3rd-party context for embedded scenarios, never solves cross-origin same-document calls cleanly, and it forces HTTPS + couples deployment.

Since browser to service is a direct HTMX call from a different origin, **auth tokens must travel as request headers**, not cookies. Services set browser-managed request headers by returning `BP-SetHeader` / `BP-RemoveHeader`. The shell stores those headers, expires them, attaches live headers to BP-managed requests, and refreshes headers that advertise `refresh=/path` before expiry.

### Auth provider services

Auth providers are standalone BSB services. Current implementations live at `services/nodejs/auth-default/` and `services/nodejs/auth-authress-io/`. An app selects one by setting `app.auth.serviceId` to a tenant service id or shared-service activation id. A provider typically exposes:

- `POST /token` - exchange credentials -> ID + refresh tokens (RS256 JWTs)
- `POST /refresh` - exchange refresh token -> new ID token
- `GET /.well-known/openid-configuration` - discovery
- `GET /.well-known/jwks.json` - public keys for ID-token verification

The auth provider is just an endpoint server. It does not maintain browser sessions, render the theme shell, or talk to the theme.

### Framework helpers

`@betterportal/framework` exports primitives:

| Export                    | Purpose                                                                 |
| ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `authLogin(opts)`         | Call the auth service's `/token` endpoint and parse the response.       |
| `authLogout(opts)`        | Call `/revoke` (when supported).                                        |
| `discoverAuthService(...)`| Resolve the app's auth provider from `bp-config` for a request.        |
| `JwksVerifier`            | Caches JWKS and verifies an ID token's signature + claims.              |
| `AuthClientError`         | Error class thrown by `authLogin` / `authLogout` for typed handling.    |
| `JwtClaims`               | Type describing the verified claim shape.                               |

Cookie helpers (`parseCookieHeader`, `serializeCookie`, `serializeClearCookie`) also exist and are useful for **same-origin** flows on the theme itself (e.g., remembering UI preferences). They are **not** part of the auth path.

### Per-route policy

Every route declares an `auth: ViewAuthRequirement`:

```ts
export const auth: ViewAuthRequirement = {
  required: false,           // true -> handler must see verified claims
  realm: "runtime",          // logical realm name
  minimumTier: "public",     // "public" | "user" | "admin" | etc.
  audiences: [],             // required JWT `aud` values
  permissions: []            // required permission strings
};
```

This is **declarative metadata only** - the framework does not enforce it. It is surfaced in `/.well-known/bp/manifest` so admin tooling and gateways can apply policy. To enforce in-service:

```ts
// In the service plugin's onRegistered:
this.app.use("/protected/**", (event) => {
  const authHeader = event.req.headers.get("authorization") ?? "";
  const token = authHeader.startsWith("Bearer ") ? authHeader.slice(7) : null;
  if (!token) return jsonResponse({ error: "unauthorized" }, 401);

  const claims = await verifier.verify(token);  // JwksVerifier instance
  (event as any).context.auth = claims;          // expose to handlers
});
```

### Recommended wiring

1. **Auth service login route** - server-rendered form/API on the auth provider service. POST returns `BP-SetHeader: Authorization=...` and any provider-specific refresh header.
2. **Header storage** - the shell owns `localStorage["bp.headers"]`; fragments and service views must not manipulate it directly.
3. **Header attach** - the shell attaches live BP headers to HTMX/fetch requests after route/service-origin rewrite and scope checks.
4. **Header refresh** - headers can include `refresh=/refresh; refreshBefore=60`. The shell POSTs the owning service with current live BP headers, applies returned BP header directives, and retries one main-content GET after a 401.
5. **Service verification** - each service uses the configured JWT verifier/middleware from app auth metadata.
6. **Route policy** - flip `auth.required = true` on protected routes. Manifests advertise this so gateways and admin tools can reason about it.

### Current state of the bootstrap1 theme

Bootstrap1 contains the shell header manager. Routes default to `required: false`; auth provider services own login/refresh/logout views and emit BP header directives.

---

## 16. Admin tooling (config-manager)

Routes mounted by config-manager:

| Path                          | Purpose                                                      |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `/services`             | Tenant service registry + wizard.                            |
| `/tenants`              | Tenants + apps CRUD.                                         |
| `/routes`               | Per-app route designer (path -> service/view binding).        |
| `/menu`                 | Tree menu editor (drag-reorder, nest, inline edit, live refresh). |
| `/fragments`            | Topbar/footer fragments editor.                              |
| `/preview`              | Component preview (per-service view inspector).              |

Backed by:
- `.../adminApi.ts` - REST CRUD for tenants/apps/routes/menu/etc. (JSON)
- `.../menuEditor.ts` - HTMX-first tree editor returning HTML fragments.
- `.../fragmentsEditor.ts` - HTMX-first fragments editor.
- `.../syncApi.ts` - SSE-based scoped config push to services.

All admin write operations emit `HX-Trigger` events so live regions in the theme refresh without a page reload.

---

## 17. Common gotchas

- **Adding a new bp-route doesn't auto-register.** You must run `bp-codegen` (or restart with a build that runs it).
- **`hx-post` to absolute URL bypasses rewriting.** Always use relative paths in JSX; let the runtime rewrite.
- **Cross-origin `HX-Trigger` requires `exposeHeaders`.** Browsers strip headers not in `Access-Control-Expose-Headers`. Update `plugins/nodejs/betterportal-bsb/src/service.ts` if you add new HX-* response headers.
- **Schema validation is strict.** Adding a new field to a config or response requires updating the `av.object({...})` and rebuilding. Unknown keys are stripped by default; tighten with `unknownKeys: "strict"` for prod.
- **Menu data model.** Top-level can be `link | group | external | section | divider`. Group children can only be non-group leaves. Theme renders one level of nesting.
- **Theme `_theme.<themeId>` directory must exist** for every theme the view wants to support. If absent, that theme cannot render the view -> fallback to embedded.
- **Don't fetch from theme code at render time.** Theme should only resolve from `bp-config.yaml` + headers. Page content comes from the browser hitting the service directly.
- **CORS preflight for PUT/DELETE.** Default `BPService` allows full HTTP method set; new custom services should not narrow it unless intentional.
- **`anyvali`** is the schema lib used everywhere. Imports as `av`. Patterns: `av.object`, `av.string`, `av.int`, `av.bool`, `av.array`, `av.enum_(["a","b"] as const)`, `av.record`, `av.optional`, `.minLength`, `.default`, `{ unknownKeys: "strip" }`.

---

## 18. Building a new service (checklist)

1. **Scaffold** the directory under `services/nodejs/<category>/<name>/`.
2. `package.json` - copy from `services/nodejs/examples/hello-view/package.json`, set `name`, `betterportal.routes` path.
3. `sec-config.yaml` - set service name + port.
4. `tsconfig.json` - extend the workspace root tsconfig.
5. **Plugin class** in `src/plugins/<plugin-name>/index.ts` extending `BPService`. Set unique `pluginId` (reverse-DNS).
6. **Routes** in `bp-routes/<routeDir>/` - put metadata in `index.ts`, method schemas/default handlers in `GET.ts`/`POST.ts`, and never export `handleGet`/`handlePost`.
7. **Theme renderers** in `bp-routes/<routeDir>/_theme.bootstrap1/GET.tsx`, `POST.tsx`, status files like `POST.422.tsx`, and any other supported theme.
8. **Fragments / components** as additional `.tsx` files in the theme dir.
9. Run `npx bp-codegen` to regenerate `.bp-generated/registry.ts`.
10. `npm run build && npm run start`.
11. Verify `curl http://localhost:<port>/.well-known/bp/manifest` returns 200.
12. **Register in `bp-config.yaml`** - add to `tenants[].services[]` (or platform-services for shared) and create at least one `apps[].routes[]` binding that points to it.
13. Restart theme + config-manager so they pick up the bp-config change (file-backed providers re-read per request, but watcher-based reloading is dev-mode only).

---

## 19. Building a new theme

1. Scaffold `themes/nodejs/<themeId>/` mirroring `themes/nodejs/bootstrap1/`.
2. Plugin class extends `BSBService` (not `BPService` - themes own their full HTTP surface).
3. Must serve:
   - The shell page (route catch-all `/**`)
   - `/.well-known/bp/manifest` + `/.well-known/bp/health`
   - Optionally `/.well-known/bp/theme/style|brand|nav|fragments` for live-refresh regions
   - Static asset endpoint (e.g., `/_themes/<themeId>/assets/**`)
4. Use `resolveThemeRequestContext(portalConfig, headers, host)` to resolve tenant + app from incoming request.
5. Use `resolveAppRoute(app, pathname)` to find the route binding for the current URL.
6. Use `buildServiceViewUrl(service, route, pathname)` to construct the absolute service URL for content swaps.
7. Inject `data-bp-services="{...origins...}"` on the shell root so `resolveServiceLinks` can rewrite paths.
8. Register the new theme in `bp-config.yaml` `themes[]` AND ensure each service has a `_theme.<themeId>/` directory for views you want it to render.

---

## 20. Quick reference - key framework exports

From `@betterportal/framework`:

| Export                                | What                                                      |
| ------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| `createHandler(schemas, fn)`          | Type-safe route handler factory.                          |
| `createBetterPortalApp()`             | New h3 app instance.                                      |
| `createBetterPortalNodeHandler(app)`  | Convert h3 app -> Node `http.Server`-compatible handler.   |
| `jsonResponse(body, status, headers)` | JSON Response with charset.                               |
| `htmlResponse(body, status, ct, h)`   | HTML Response with charset.                               |
| `handleCorsRequest(event, opts)`      | h3 CORS helper (with method + expose-headers).            |
| `registerServiceConfigRoutes(opts)`   | Adds `/.well-known/bp/config*` routes to a service.       |
| `resolveThemeRequestContext(...)`     | Map incoming request to `{ tenant, app }` from bp-config. |
| `resolveAppRoute(app, pathname)`      | Match URL -> route binding.                                |
| `resolveServiceForTenant(...)`        | Resolve `serviceId` to its tenant binding.                |
| `buildServiceViewUrl(svc, route, p)`  | Construct absolute service URL.                           |
| `FileBackedBetterPortalConfigProvider`| Reads bp-config.yaml.                                     |
| `FileBackedPlatformConfigStore`       | Read + write bp-config.yaml.                              |
| `InMemoryServiceConfigStore`          | Dev config storage for per-service settings.              |
| `FileBackedServiceConfigStore`        | Encrypted persistent config storage.                      |
| `generateApiKey()`, `hashApiKey(k)`   | API key + SHA-256 hashing for service registrations.      |
| `uuidv7()`                            | UUIDv7 generator.                                         |
| `toHtmlString(renderable)`            | Convert jsx-htmx output to string.                        |

From `@betterportal/plugin-bsb`:

| Export                                | What                                                      |
| ------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| `BPService`                           | Service plugin base class (extends `BSBService`).         |
| `BPServiceDefinition`                 | Return type of `definition()` - `{ manifest, registry }`. |
| `createBsbObservability(obs)`         | Adapter from BSB's `Observable` -> framework observability.|

From `@bsb/base`:

| Export                                | What                                                      |
| ------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| `BSBService`                          | Base service class.                                       |
| `BSBServiceConstructor<C, E>`         | Constructor argument type (Config + EventSchemas).        |
| `createConfigSchema(meta, schema)`    | Wrap an `av` schema as a BSB Config class.                |
| `createEventSchemas({ ... })`         | Declare emit / on / broadcast event shapes.               |
| `Observable`                          | BSB observability handle (log, span, metric).             |

---

## 21. When in doubt

- Read `services/nodejs/examples/hello-view/` - the canonical "hello world" service.
- Read `services/nodejs/admin/config-manager/` - the most complete real service (admin tooling, HTMX editors, sync).
- Read `themes/nodejs/bootstrap1/src/plugins/.../index.ts` - host page rendering, nav/style/brand/fragment endpoints, theme designer + live-refresh hookups, auth helper imports (not yet wired into a flow).
- `docs/architecture/overview.md` for principles.
- `docs/decisions/*.md` for ADRs.
